majors.count_method: official Duke 'Majors, Minors, and More' page states Duke offers 63 majors, 61 minors, and 23 certificates
titles: Program II (self-designed degree, BA or BS)
Spanish, Latin American, and Latino/a Studies
majors.notes: official Duke 'Majors, Minors, and More' page states Duke offers 63 majors, 61 minors, and 23 certificates; titles extracted from official page: https://admissions.duke.edu/academic-possibilities/

admissions.testing_policy: Duke is test-optional for first-year and transfer applicants in the 2026-2027 admissions cycle.
admissions.gpa_policy: Duke does not publish a minimum GPA on the cited admissions pages.
admissions.course_rigor: Duke says it is guided by the rigor of a candidate's academic program and generally expects students to enroll in five academic courses per year, taking the best available and most challenging courses.
admissions.recommendations: Duke requires a secondary school report with counselor recommendation and two teacher recommendations.
admissions.essays: Duke requires a one-page personal essay and short-answer questions specific to Duke.

projects_research: Duke highlights undergraduate research as an academic opportunity and says extracurricular activities are one of its five primary review factors.

evidence.source_excerpt: Duke University offers 63 majors, 61 minors, and 23 certificates.

evidence.claim: Duke is test-optional for first-year and transfer applicants in the 2026-2027 admissions cycle.
evidence.source_excerpt: Duke University is test-optional for both first-year and transfer applicants in the 2026-2027 admissions cycle. Students who apply without SAT or ACT scores this year will not be at a disadvantage.

evidence.claim: Duke says it is guided by the rigor of a candidate's academic program and generally expects students to enroll in five academic courses per year, taking the best available and most challenging courses.
evidence.source_excerpt: The rigor of a candidate's academic program ... Enroll in the best available and most challenging courses. We generally expect students to enroll in five academic courses per year.

evidence.claim: Duke requires a secondary school report with counselor recommendation and two teacher recommendations.
evidence.source_excerpt: Secondary School Report with Counselor Recommendation Two Teacher Recommendations

evidence.claim: Duke requires a one-page personal essay and short-answer questions specific to Duke.
evidence.source_excerpt: Applications for admission require a one-page personal essay, along with short-answer questions specific to Duke.

evidence.claim: Duke highlights undergraduate research as an academic opportunity and says extracurricular activities are one of its five primary review factors.
evidence.source_excerpt: Academic Opportunities ... Undergraduate Research ... Duke says extracurricular activities are one of its five primary review factors.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 professors from RateMyProfessors by rating count and sentiment metrics.
evidence.source_excerpt: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Duke University' (legacyId=1350) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 notable alumni from Wikipedia-derived candidate lists using trailing pageviews.
evidence.source_excerpt: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, and ranked by pageviews. Major-specific and last-10-years filtering requires stronger school-specific alumni sources than this generic pipeline currently has.

African & African American Studies; Art History; Art History: Architecture; Art History: Museum Theory & Practice; Art History & Visual Arts; Asian and Middle Eastern Studies; Biology; Biomedical Engineering; Biophysics; Brazilian and Global Portuguese Studies; Chemistry; Civil Engineering

warnings: Major titles extracted from official school source (64 titles).
Phase 4 structured upgrade applied to top-10 expansion schools.
School-people enrichment uses public third-party sources (RateMyProfessors and Wikipedia/Wikimedia), so rankings are heuristic rather than official university data.

popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Duke University' (legacyId=1350) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official Duke Mag Young & Rising profiles, prioritizing person-specific Duke profile pages that explicitly identify alumni class years and describe substantive achievement in science, health, technology, public service, sports, media, business, and the arts.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official Duke Mag profile pages on dukemag.duke.edu, especially the Young & Rising collection.

items.name: Gabriela Asturias
items.bio: Duke Mag profiles Gabriela Asturias ’17 as a physician-scientist in psychiatry and a global mental health entrepreneur focused on building scalable, community-based mental health systems in low- and middle-income countries, especially Guatemala, while serving as a Stanford psychiatry resident and co-founding MiResource and FUNDEGUA.
items.description: Physician-scientist in psychiatry and global mental health entrepreneur.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Duke Mag person-specific profile page with explicit Duke class-year evidence and a substantive career summary.

items.name: Brinnae Bent
items.bio: Duke Mag profiles Brinnae Bent M.S.’18, Ph.D.’21 as Duke faculty, director of the TRUST Lab, associate director of the Society-Centered AI Initiative, and executive director of Tensor & Trust.
items.description: Duke AI faculty leader and TRUST Lab director.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Duke Mag person-specific profile page with explicit Duke class-year evidence and a substantive career summary.

items.name: Lexie Brown
items.bio: Duke Mag profiles Lexie Brown ’17, M.M.S.’18 as an eight-year WNBA veteran, member of the 2021 champion Chicago Sky, co-host of the podcast “Full Circle,” chairperson of Athletes Unlimited Pro Basketball, and player representative for the Women’s National Basketball Players Association.
items.description: WNBA veteran and women’s basketball leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Duke Mag person-specific profile page with explicit Duke class-year evidence and a substantive career summary.

items.name: Stefanie Feldman
items.bio: Duke Mag profiles Stefanie Feldman ’10 as national policy director for Joe Biden’s 2020 presidential campaign and a senior policy advisor in the Biden White House.
items.description: National policy strategist and White House advisor.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Duke Mag person-specific profile page with explicit Duke class-year evidence and a substantive career summary.

items.name: Eric Oberstein
items.bio: Duke Mag profiles Eric Oberstein ’07 as a multiple Grammy- and Latin Grammy-winning producer, artist manager, curator, consultant, and educator who runs Eric Oberstein Productions and also teaches at Teachers College, Columbia University, and George Mason University.
items.description: Grammy-winning producer and arts manager.
items.major: Cultural Anthropology; certificates in Arts Management & Cultural Policy and Latin American Studies
